entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum L., the host crop affected by Ascochyta blight

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA BLIGHT
entities.description: The most destructive disease of chickpea caused by the fungus Ascochyta rabiei

entities.name: ASCOCHYTA RABIEI
entities.description: The fungus that causes Ascochyta blight

entities.name: LOW TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental condition between 15-25ºC that favors the development of Ascochyta blight

entities.name: HIGH HUMIDITY
entities.description: Environmental condition with rainfall greater than 150mm that favors the development of Ascochyta blight

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region where the Ascochyta rabiei isolates were studied

entities.name: PATHOTYPE
entities.description: Classification of fungal isolates based on their aggressiveness

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL RACE
entities.description: Classification of fungal isolates based on their virulence

entities.name: AGGRESSIVENESS
entities.description: The characteristic used to classify isolates into pathotypes

entities.name: VIRULENCE
entities.description: The characteristic used to classify isolates into physiological races

entities.name: CHICKPEA DIFFERENTIALS
entities.description: Standard international chickpea lines used to identify pathotypes and physiological races

entities.name: MULTIPLEX PCR
entities.description: Molecular technique used to identify mating types in Ascochyta rabiei isolates

entities.name: MAT1-2
entities.description: The specific mating type identified in Indian Ascochyta rabiei isolates

entities.name: HAEMOCYTOMETER
entities.description: Instrument used to adjust the concentration of spore suspensions
